channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge dismisses charges in alleged campus vigilante 'Catch a Predator' sting targeting Army soldier
item.description: Five Massachusetts college students accused of attacking a man in a "To Catch a Predator" sting have had their kidnapping and conspiracy charges dropped.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pressed for evidence against Mahmoud Khalil, government cites its power to deport people for beliefs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Facing a deadline from an immigration judge to turn over evidence for its attempted deportation of Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil, the federal government has instead submitted a brief memo, signed by Secretary of State Marco Rubio, citing the Trump administration’s autho
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USPS warns about common scams
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service and Postal Inspection Service want to warn consumers to be aware of fraud and spam regarding their mail, and what to do to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Movie theaters erupt in chaos as part of 'Minecraft' social media trend
item.description: Rowdy crowds of young people have been disrupting screenings of the newly released "A Minecraft Movie" around the country as they take part in the so-called "chicken jockey" trend.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: Hearing looks at rules for evidence and other details in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Prosecutors and attorneys for a man charged in the killings of four University of Idaho students in 2022 argued some of the final ground rules they want for Bryan Kohberger's trial in a hearing Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Homeland Security agents denied entry at two Los Angeles schools
item.description: It is believed to be the first time federal agents tried to enter Los Angeles schools since President Trump announced a crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Newly surfaced report provides more clues on COVID outbreak source and timeline
item.description: A newly released report reveals seven U.S. military athletes showed COVID-19 symptoms after the 2019 Military World Games in Wuhan, China, contradicting official timeline. New evidence fuels debate on virus origins.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds of students, dozens of colleges hit by Trump's visa purge: What to know
item.description: International students are seeing their visas revoked for infractions as minor as traffic violations.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Bill could change requirements for arresting students who make a threat of violence
item.description: A state law makes mass violence threats against schools a felony — regardless of intent or ability to carry them out.
item.dc:creator: Kori Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans pass bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
item.description: House Republicans for a second time passed a stand-alone bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections and impose voter roll purge requirements on states. The legislation — formally titled the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act — passed in a 220-208 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Uber releases list of the strangest things left in its cars
item.description: It's no surprise that phones, wallets, and keys are the most common items left behind in Uber cars. But a urinal and a taxidermied rabbit? Uber has released its 2025 "Lost & Found Index," listing the 50 most unique items left behind in its rideshare vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: House GOP adopts Trump budget blueprint after last-minute scramble
item.description: House Republicans on Thursday adopted the Senate’s framework that will be used to enact key parts of President Trump’s legislative agenda, getting the blueprint over the finish line after a last-minute scramble to win over conservatives who had spent days railing against the measure.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: Residents in this Florida neighborhood are under attack -- from hawks
item.description: A Florida woman has a unique problem outside of her Sarasota business. A pair of nesting hawks is attacking anyone who walks through the front yard. "Everybody, the minute you go in the walkway here, you get attacked," business owner Odalys Hayes said.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, KTLA 5

item.title: Jury awards $1.68 billion to women who accused writer and director James Toback of sexual abuse
item.description: The judgement marks one of the largest jury awards since the advent of the #MeToo movement, as well as in New York state history
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, Fox 5

item.title: This federal rule helped clear air over America's most beloved parks. Trump's EPA wants to kill it
item.description: During a hike in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park in 1995, Don Barger climbed Chilhowee Mountain hoping to gaze across the valley below. All he saw was a wall of gray haze. Today, he said, he can see some 50 miles (80 kilometers) across that same valley to the Cumberland Mountains.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND and MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dolphins player Tyreek Hill and his wife were involved in a domestic dispute, police say
item.description: SUNNY ISLES BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Miami Dolphins wide receiver Tyreek Hill and his wife were involved in a domestic dispute at their South Florida high-rise condominium, but no charges were filed, according to a police report.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas lottery player claims he was cheated out of $95 million jackpot win
item.description: A Texas lottery player filed a lawsuit in Travis County on Tuesday alleging his May 2023 Lotto Texas jackpot prize was severely diminished by $95 million because a group of lottery retailers and a London-based sports gambling company conspired to rig a lottery drawing less than a month before his wi
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, KRON4

item.title: Tips for spring gardening: Utah experts talk best times to prep and plant
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- Gardening can be many things to different people—sometimes a chore, other times a passion. If you're one of those passionate gardeners or someone looking to get started, here are some tips from experts at Millcreek Gardens to help you get your garden started.
item.dc:creator: Nate Larsen, ABC 4

item.title: 1,700 pounds of butter recalled over bacteria contamination
item.description: Nearly a ton of salted butter is being recalled because, according to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, the product is "contaminated with elevated levels of coliform," a bacteria found in animals' digestive tracts and waste.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp
item.description: The increase for a “forever” stamp, postcards, metered letters and international mail would take effect this summer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: She survived being shot in the head as teen. Now she's sharing her final message
item.description: Deserae Turner — who survived being shot in the head in 2017 and has undergone several medical procedures since — is now in hospice.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, Fox 5

item.title: LeBron James becomes first pro male athlete to have likeness depicted in Ken doll
item.description: LeBron James has become the first professional male athlete to have his likeness depicted in a Ken doll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 4 charged after Virginia shooting that killed 3, authorities say
item.description: Four people were charged Wednesday in connection with a shooting that killed three people and wounded three others in Virginia, authorities said. Three suspects have been arrested on charges of malicious wounding and felony firearm use.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NYC lets immigration officials open an office at Rikers jail, a priority for Trump
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams ' administration is allowing federal immigration officials to operate at Rikers Island to work on gang and drug-related criminal investigations in the city's largest lockup.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The world's most famous YouTuber slams Trump's tariff war
item.description: Entrepreneur and YouTube star MrBeast slammed President Trump’s “brutal” tariffs in a post on the social platform X on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: Wall Street surges to one of its best days since WWII after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks soared to one of their best days in history on a euphoric Wall Street Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would back off on most of his tariffs temporarily, as investors had so desperately hoped he would.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job: Shopify CEO
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, Fox 5

item.title: Wall Street surges to one of its best days since WWII after Trump pauses most tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks soared to one of their best days in history on a euphoric Wall Street Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would back off on most of his tariffs temporarily, as investors had so desperately hoped he would.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to fine migrants up to $1K daily for defying deportation order
item.description: The administration is planning to retroactively apply the fines for up to five years, which means some immigrants could owe more than $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, WGN 9

item.title: New Mexico governor mobilizes National Guard to tackle crime emergency in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham on Tuesday declared a state of emergency in New Mexico’s largest city, saying that a significant increase in crime in Albuquerque warrants the help of the New Mexico National Guard.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: Idaho police officers opened fire from behind a chain-link fence just seconds after exiting their patrol cars and critically wounded a teenage boy — described by his family as nonverbal, autistic and intellectually disabled — as he stepped toward them with a knife, video from a witness shows.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, CLAUDIA LAUER and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Youth soccer coach charged in the death of 13-year-old found on Southern California road
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A youth soccer coach has been charged in the death of a 13-year-old whose body was found on the side of a Southern California road.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Surgery nightmare: Hospital admits mistake after failed cancer surgery attempt
item.description: An investigation is underway at MountainView Hospital in Las Vegas after a cancer patient woke up to startling news in the operating room.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Murphy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A Florida man is executed for killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break was executed Tuesday evening. Michael Tanzi was pronounced dead at 6:12 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Photos: 'Jackson Magnolia' removed at White House, replaced with descendant sapling
item.description: The newest greenery added to the White House grounds came the day after one of the southern magnolia trees, which was said to have been planted by Jackson in the 19th century, was removed due to "serious safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5
